reasoning: The forecast date is November 9, 2025, leaving less than two months until the resolution date of January 1, 2026. Moving an emergence score from its recent levels (like 5.79 for H5N1 in late 2024) to >= 8.0 ("high") would necessitate a severe escalation in transmissibility characteristics, followed by an official evaluation and publication from the CDC, all before the end of the year.
